2	
3	# Set extension name here
4	set(TARGET_NAME jq)
5	
6	# DuckDB's extension distribution supports vcpkg. As such, dependencies can be added in ./vcpkg.json and then
7	# used in cmake with find_package. Feel free to remove or replace with other dependencies.
8	# Note that it should also be removed from vcpkg.json to prevent needlessly installing it..
9	find_package(OpenSSL REQUIRED)
10	
11	set(EXTENSION_NAME ${TARGET_NAME}_extension)
12	set(LOADABLE_EXTENSION_NAME ${TARGET_NAME}_loadable_extension)
13	
14	project(${TARGET_NAME})
15	
16	set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD "17" CACHE STRING "C++ standard to enforce")
17	set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
18	
19	include_directories(src/include)
20	
21	set(EXTENSION_SOURCES src/jq_extension.cpp)
22	
23	build_static_extension(${TARGET_NAME} ${EXTENSION_SOURCES})
24	build_loadable_extension(${TARGET_NAME} " " ${EXTENSION_SOURCES})
25	
26	# Link OpenSSL in both the static library as the loadable extension
27	target_link_libraries(${EXTENSION_NAME} OpenSSL::SSL OpenSSL::Crypto)
28	target_link_libraries(${LOADABLE_EXTENSION_NAME} OpenSSL::SSL OpenSSL::Crypto)
29	
30	install(
31	  TARGETS ${EXTENSION_NAME}
32	  EXPORT "${DUCKDB_EXPORT_SET}"
33	  LIBRARY DESTINATION "${INSTALL_LIB_DIR}"
34	  ARCHIVE DESTINATION "${INSTALL_LIB_DIR}")
35

file.content:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.5)

# Set extension name here
set(TARGET_NAME jq)

# DuckDB's extension distribution supports vcpkg. As such, dependencies can be added in ./vcpkg.json and then
# used in cmake with find_package. Feel free to remove or replace with other dependencies.
# Note that it should also be removed from vcpkg.json to prevent needlessly installing it..
find_package(OpenSSL REQUIRED)

set(EXTENSION_NAME ${TARGET_NAME}_extension)
set(LOADABLE_EXTENSION_NAME ${TARGET_NAME}_loadable_extension)

project(${TARGET_NAME})

set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD "17" CACHE STRING "C++ standard to enforce")
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)

include_directories(src/include)

set(EXTENSION_SOURCES src/jq_extension.cpp)

build_static_extension(${TARGET_NAME} ${EXTENSION_SOURCES})
build_loadable_extension(${TARGET_NAME} " " ${EXTENSION_SOURCES})

# Link OpenSSL in both the static library as the loadable extension
target_link_libraries(${EXTENSION_NAME} OpenSSL::SSL OpenSSL::Crypto)
target_link_libraries(${LOADABLE_EXTENSION_NAME} OpenSSL::SSL OpenSSL::Crypto)

install(
  TARGETS ${EXTENSION_NAME}
  EXPORT "${DUCKDB_EXPORT_SET}"
  LIBRARY DESTINATION "${INSTALL_LIB_DIR}"
  ARCHIVE DESTINATION "${INSTALL_LIB_DIR}")

content.thinking:
I'm considering how to integrate jq into a CMake project. The main options are either building it through CMake using external project tools with autotools invocation at build time, or directly compiling the source files as a library to skip autotools entirely, though the second approach has some complications I need to work through.

Looking at what's actually in the jq repository, I can see that the generated parser, lexer, and builtin files are already committed, but I need to understand what other generated files like config.h are required and how the build process depends on them.

2	### C source files to be built and distributed.
3	
4	LIBJQ_INCS = src/builtin.h src/bytecode.h src/compile.h                 \
5	        src/exec_stack.h src/jq_parser.h src/jv_alloc.h src/jv_dtoa.h   \
6	        src/jv_unicode.h src/jv_utf8_tables.h src/lexer.l src/libm.h    \
7	        src/linker.h src/locfile.h src/opcode_list.h src/parser.y       \
8	        src/util.h src/jv_dtoa_tsd.h src/jv_thread.h src/jv_private.h   \
9	        vendor/decNumber/decContext.h vendor/decNumber/decNumber.h      \
10	        vendor/decNumber/decNumberLocal.h
11	
12	LIBJQ_SRC = src/builtin.c src/bytecode.c src/compile.c src/execute.c    \
13	        src/jq_test.c src/jv.c src/jv_alloc.c src/jv_aux.c              \
14	        src/jv_dtoa.c src/jv_file.c src/jv_parse.c src/jv_print.c       \
15	        src/jv_unicode.c src/linker.c src/locfile.c src/util.c          \
16	        src/jv_dtoa_tsd.c                                               \
17	        vendor/decNumber/decContext.c vendor/decNumber/decNumber.c      \
18	        ${LIBJQ_INCS}
19	
20	### C build options
21	
22	AM_CFLAGS = -Wextra -Wall -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-unused-function \
23	            -Woverlength-strings -Wstrict-prototypes
24	
25	if WIN32
26	AM_CFLAGS += -municode
27	endif
28	
29	ACLOCAL_AMFLAGS = -I config/m4
30	
31	### Generating the lexer and parser
32	
33	# While there is some autoconf macro support for lex/flex, it doesn't support
34	# header file creation so we'll use good old make
35	if MAINTAINER_MODE
36	BUILT_SOURCES = src/lexer.h src/lexer.c src/parser.h src/parser.c \
37	                src/builtin.inc src/config_opts.inc src/version.h
38	src/lexer.c: src/lexer.l
39		$(AM_V_LEX) flex -o src/lexer.c --header-file=src/lexer.h $<
40	src/lexer.h: src/lexer.c
41	else
42	BUILT_SOURCES = src/builtin.inc src/config_opts.inc src/version.h
43	.y.c:
44		$(AM_V_YACC) echo "NOT building parser.c!"
45	.l.c:
46		$(AM_V_LEX) echo "NOT building lexer.c!"
47	endif
48	
49	# Tell YACC (Bison) autoconf macros that you want a header file created.
50	# If the --warnings=all fails, you probably have an old version of Bison
51	# macOS ships an old Bison, so update with Homebrew or MacPorts.
52	AM_YFLAGS = --warnings=all -Wno-yacc -d
53	
54	### libjq
55	
56	lib_LTLIBRARIES = libjq.la
57	libjq_la_SOURCES = ${LIBJQ_SRC}
58	libjq_la_LIBADD = -lm
59	libjq_la_LDFLAGS = $(onig_LDFLAGS) -export-symbols-regex '^j[qv]_' -version-info 1:4:0
60	
61	if WIN32
62	libjq_la_LIBADD += -lshlwapi
63	libjq_la_LDFLAGS += -no-undefined
64	endif
65	
66	include_HEADERS = src/jv.h src/jq.h
67	
68	AM_CPPFLAGS = -I$(srcdir)/src -I$(srcdir)/vendor
69	
70	### Address sanitizer (ASan)
71	
72	if ENABLE_ASAN
73	AM_CFLAGS += -fsanitize=address
74	endif
75	
76	### Undefined Behavior Sanitizer
77	
78	if ENABLE_UBSAN
79	AM_CFLAGS += -fsanitize=undefined
80	endif
81	
82	### Code coverage with gcov
83	
84	if ENABLE_GCOV
85	AM_CFLAGS += --coverage --no-inline
86	endif
87	
88	### Error injection for testing
89	
90	if ENABLE_ERROR_INJECTION
91	lib_LTLIBRARIES += libinject_errors.la
92	libinject_errors_la_SOURCES = src/inject_errors.c
93	libinject_errors_la_LIBADD = -ldl
94	libinject_errors_la_LDFLAGS = -module
95	endif
96	
97	### Building the jq binary
98	
99	# Remake the version.h header file if, and only if, the git ID has changed
100	.PHONY: .FORCE

file.content:
### C source files to be built and distributed.

LIBJQ_INCS = src/builtin.h src/bytecode.h src/compile.h                 \
        src/exec_stack.h src/jq_parser.h src/jv_alloc.h src/jv_dtoa.h   \
        src/jv_unicode.h src/jv_utf8_tables.h src/lexer.l src/libm.h    \
        src/linker.h src/locfile.h src/opcode_list.h src/parser.y       \
        src/util.h src/jv_dtoa_tsd.h src/jv_thread.h src/jv_private.h   \
        vendor/decNumber/decContext.h vendor/decNumber/decNumber.h      \
        vendor/decNumber/decNumberLocal.h

LIBJQ_SRC = src/builtin.c src/bytecode.c src/compile.c src/execute.c    \
        src/jq_test.c src/jv.c src/jv_alloc.c src/jv_aux.c              \
        src/jv_dtoa.c src/jv_file.c src/jv_parse.c src/jv_print.c       \
        src/jv_unicode.c src/linker.c src/locfile.c src/util.c          \
        src/jv_dtoa_tsd.c                                               \
        vendor/decNumber/decContext.c vendor/decNumber/decNumber.c      \
        ${LIBJQ_INCS}

### C build options

AM_CFLAGS = -Wextra -Wall -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-unused-function \
            -Woverlength-strings -Wstrict-prototypes

if WIN32
AM_CFLAGS += -municode
endif

ACLOCAL_AMFLAGS = -I config/m4

### Generating the lexer and parser

# While there is some autoconf macro support for lex/flex, it doesn't support
# header file creation so we'll use good old make
if MAINTAINER_MODE
BUILT_SOURCES = src/lexer.h src/lexer.c src/parser.h src/parser.c \
                src/builtin.inc src/config_opts.inc src/version.h
src/lexer.c: src/lexer.l
	$(AM_V_LEX) flex -o src/lexer.c --header-file=src/lexer.h $<
src/lexer.h: src/lexer.c
else
BUILT_SOURCES = src/builtin.inc src/config_opts.inc src/version.h
.y.c:
	$(AM_V_YACC) echo "NOT building parser.c!"
.l.c:
	$(AM_V_LEX) echo "NOT building lexer.c!"
endif

# Tell YACC (Bison) autoconf macros that you want a header file created.
# If the --warnings=all fails, you probably have an old version of Bison
# macOS ships an old Bison, so update with Homebrew or MacPorts.
AM_YFLAGS = --warnings=all -Wno-yacc -d

### libjq

lib_LTLIBRARIES = libjq.la
libjq_la_SOURCES = ${LIBJQ_SRC}
libjq_la_LIBADD = -lm
libjq_la_LDFLAGS = $(onig_LDFLAGS) -export-symbols-regex '^j[qv]_' -version-info 1:4:0

if WIN32
libjq_la_LIBADD += -lshlwapi
libjq_la_LDFLAGS += -no-undefined
endif

include_HEADERS = src/jv.h src/jq.h

AM_CPPFLAGS = -I$(srcdir)/src -I$(srcdir)/vendor

### Address sanitizer (ASan)

if ENABLE_ASAN
AM_CFLAGS += -fsanitize=address
endif

### Undefined Behavior Sanitizer

if ENABLE_UBSAN
AM_CFLAGS += -fsanitize=undefined
endif

### Code coverage with gcov

if ENABLE_GCOV
AM_CFLAGS += --coverage --no-inline
endif

### Error injection for testing

if ENABLE_ERROR_INJECTION
lib_LTLIBRARIES += libinject_errors.la
libinject_errors_la_SOURCES = src/inject_errors.c
libinject_errors_la_LIBADD = -ldl
libinject_errors_la_LDFLAGS = -module
endif

### Building the jq binary

# Remake the version.h header file if, and only if, the git ID has changed
.PHONY: .FORCE

content.thinking:
Looking at jq's build system, it relies on autotools and has a dependency on the oniguruma regex library that gets linked in. The build generates several key files like builtin.inc, config_opts.inc, and version.h, while the parser and lexer are typically pre-generated from their source definitions in non-maintainer builds.

For integrating this into a DuckDB extension with CMake, the most practical approach is using ExternalProject_Add to invoke jq's autotools build chain directly—running autoreconf, configure, and make to produce the static library. Since jq is already set up as a submodule, this avoids having to manually replicate the complex build logic. Setting up the imported library target...

The issue is that modifying the source directory during the build will dirty the submodule, so I need to use an out-of-source build pattern instead where the build happens in a separate directory to keep things clean.

I should also consider jq's configure options — `--without-oniguruma` removes regex support for a smaller binary, but the user might want that functionality. The build will need autoreconf to generate the necessary files like `builtin.inc` and `version.h` before make runs, which ExternalProject_Add handles automatically.

content.text: Quick reality check on what's involved: jq uses Autotools (configure.ac, Makefile.am) and depends on generated files (`src/builtin.inc`, `src/config_opts.inc`, `src/version.h`) and optionally on oniguruma for regex. So you can't get away with a plain `add_library(jq STATIC <list of .c files>)` — the build needs to run `autoreconf && configure && make` to produce those generated headers first.

1	m4_define([jq_version], m4_esyscmd_s([scripts/version])))
2	
3	AC_INIT([jq],[jq_version],[https://github.com/jqlang/jq/issues],[jq],[https://jqlang.org])
4	
5	dnl Created autoconf implementation thompson@dtosolutions, 26NOV12
6	AC_PREREQ([2.65])
7	AC_CONFIG_AUX_DIR([config])
8	AC_USE_SYSTEM_EXTENSIONS
9	AM_INIT_AUTOMAKE([1.11.2 subdir-objects parallel-tests foreign -Wall])
10	AM_SILENT_RULES([yes])
11	AM_PROG_AR
12	AM_MAINTAINER_MODE([disable])
13	AC_PROG_CC
14	m4_version_prereq([2.70], [], [AC_PROG_CC_STDC])
15	AC_PROG_CPP_WERROR
16	AC_PROG_YACC
17	AC_OBJEXT
18	AC_EXEEXT
19	LT_INIT([shared static win32-dll])
20	AM_PROG_CC_C_O
21	
22	AC_SYS_LARGEFILE # issue 2167
23	
24	dnl couldn't use AM_PROG_LEX as it doesn't support header files like the
25	dnl AC_PROG_YACC macros...
26	
27	dnl check bison version
28	
29	if test "$USE_MAINTAINER_MODE" = yes; then
30	  if test "$YACC" != "bison -y"; then
31	    AC_MSG_CHECKING([bison version])
32	    AC_MSG_RESULT([not bison])
33	  else
34	    AX_PROG_BISON_VERSION([3],
35	      [],
36	      [AC_MSG_ERROR([You need bison version 3.0 or greater])])
37	  fi
38	
39	  AC_CHECK_PROGS(LEX, flex lex)
40	fi
41	
42	AC_CHECK_FUNCS(memmem)
43	
44	AC_CHECK_HEADER("sys/cygwin.h", [have_cygwin=1;])
45	AC_CHECK_HEADER("shlwapi.h",[have_shlwapi=1;])
46	AM_CONDITIONAL([WIN32], [test "x$have_shlwapi" = x1 && test ! "x$have_cygwin" = x1])
47	
48	dnl Running tests with Valgrind is slow. It is faster to iterate on
49	dnl code without Valgrind until tests pass, then enable Valgrind and
50	dnl fix leaks.
51	AC_ARG_ENABLE([valgrind],
52	   AS_HELP_STRING([--enable-valgrind],[enable Valgrind during testing]))
53	
54	dnl Address sanitizer (ASan)
55	AC_ARG_ENABLE([asan],
56	   AS_HELP_STRING([--enable-asan],[enable address sanitizer]))
57	
58	dnl Undefined Behavior Sanitizer
59	AC_ARG_ENABLE([ubsan],
60	   AS_HELP_STRING([--enable-ubsan],[enable undefined behavior sanitizer]))
61	
62	dnl Code coverage
63	AC_ARG_ENABLE([gcov],
64	   AS_HELP_STRING([--enable-gcov],[enable gcov code coverage tool]))
65	
66	dnl Don't attempt to build docs if python deps aren't installed
67	AC_ARG_ENABLE([docs],
68	   AS_HELP_STRING([--disable-docs],[do not build docs]), [], [enable_docs=yes])
69	
70	dnl Don't attempt to build the error injection object (if there is no LD_PRELOAD support)
71	AC_ARG_ENABLE([error-injection],
72	   AS_HELP_STRING([--enable-error-injection],[build and test with error injection]))
73	
74	dnl Enable building all static
75	AC_ARG_ENABLE([all-static],
76	   AS_HELP_STRING([--enable-all-static],[link jq with static libraries only]))
77	
78	dnl find pipenv
79	AC_ARG_VAR([PIPENV], [pipenv command])
80	AC_CHECK_PROGS([PIPENV], pipenv)

file.content:
m4_define([jq_version], m4_esyscmd_s([scripts/version])))

AC_INIT([jq],[jq_version],[https://github.com/jqlang/jq/issues],[jq],[https://jqlang.org])

dnl Created autoconf implementation thompson@dtosolutions, 26NOV12
AC_PREREQ([2.65])
AC_CONFIG_AUX_DIR([config])
AC_USE_SYSTEM_EXTENSIONS
AM_INIT_AUTOMAKE([1.11.2 subdir-objects parallel-tests foreign -Wall])
AM_SILENT_RULES([yes])
AM_PROG_AR
AM_MAINTAINER_MODE([disable])
AC_PROG_CC
m4_version_prereq([2.70], [], [AC_PROG_CC_STDC])
AC_PROG_CPP_WERROR
AC_PROG_YACC
AC_OBJEXT
AC_EXEEXT
LT_INIT([shared static win32-dll])
AM_PROG_CC_C_O

AC_SYS_LARGEFILE # issue 2167

dnl couldn't use AM_PROG_LEX as it doesn't support header files like the
dnl AC_PROG_YACC macros...

dnl check bison version

if test "$USE_MAINTAINER_MODE" = yes; then
  if test "$YACC" != "bison -y"; then
    AC_MSG_CHECKING([bison version])
    AC_MSG_RESULT([not bison])
  else
    AX_PROG_BISON_VERSION([3],
      [],
      [AC_MSG_ERROR([You need bison version 3.0 or greater])])
  fi

  AC_CHECK_PROGS(LEX, flex lex)
fi

AC_CHECK_FUNCS(memmem)

AC_CHECK_HEADER("sys/cygwin.h", [have_cygwin=1;])
AC_CHECK_HEADER("shlwapi.h",[have_shlwapi=1;])
AM_CONDITIONAL([WIN32], [test "x$have_shlwapi" = x1 && test ! "x$have_cygwin" = x1])

dnl Running tests with Valgrind is slow. It is faster to iterate on
dnl code without Valgrind until tests pass, then enable Valgrind and
dnl fix leaks.
AC_ARG_ENABLE([valgrind],
   AS_HELP_STRING([--enable-valgrind],[enable Valgrind during testing]))

dnl Address sanitizer (ASan)
AC_ARG_ENABLE([asan],
   AS_HELP_STRING([--enable-asan],[enable address sanitizer]))

dnl Undefined Behavior Sanitizer
AC_ARG_ENABLE([ubsan],
   AS_HELP_STRING([--enable-ubsan],[enable undefined behavior sanitizer]))

dnl Code coverage
AC_ARG_ENABLE([gcov],
   AS_HELP_STRING([--enable-gcov],[enable gcov code coverage tool]))

dnl Don't attempt to build docs if python deps aren't installed
AC_ARG_ENABLE([docs],
   AS_HELP_STRING([--disable-docs],[do not build docs]), [], [enable_docs=yes])

dnl Don't attempt to build the error injection object (if there is no LD_PRELOAD support)
AC_ARG_ENABLE([error-injection],
   AS_HELP_STRING([--enable-error-injection],[build and test with error injection]))

dnl Enable building all static
AC_ARG_ENABLE([all-static],
   AS_HELP_STRING([--enable-all-static],[link jq with static libraries only]))

dnl find pipenv
AC_ARG_VAR([PIPENV], [pipenv command])
AC_CHECK_PROGS([PIPENV], pipenv)

lastPrompt: Can you help me figure out how to make "jq.h" / libjq available from jq_extension.cpp? I've checked out the jq git repository as a submodule in `./jq`. I suspect it's something like `add_library(...)`…

content.thinking:
I'm going to recommend using `ExternalProject_Add` to build jq's autotools setup out-of-tree, which will produce the static library and headers that can then be imported as a CMake target — this keeps things clean and self-contained.

The key configuration flags are: disable shared libraries and enable static with position-independent code (since the static lib gets linked into a DuckDB .so/.dylib), optionally skip oniguruma regex support or use the vendored version, and disable maintainer mode and docs to avoid needing yacc/flex. I'll also need to specify `BUILD_BYPRODUCTS` so the build system knows where the outputs are, then create the imported target and wire up the linking.

Writing the CMake patch...

content.text:
(Skipping task list — this is a short Q&A.)

Recommendation: drive jq's autotools build via `ExternalProject_Add`, install it to a build-dir prefix, and import the resulting `libjq.a` as a `STATIC IMPORTED` target.

Why not `add_library(jq STATIC <sources>)`: jq needs `src/builtin.inc`, `src/config_opts.inc`, and `src/version.h` generated by configure/make, plus optional oniguruma — replicating that in CMake is more work than just calling jq's own build.

Concrete edit to `CMakeLists.txt`:

input.old_string:
include_directories(src/include)

set(EXTENSION_SOURCES src/jq_extension.cpp)

build_static_extension(${TARGET_NAME} ${EXTENSION_SOURCES})
build_loadable_extension(${TARGET_NAME} " " ${EXTENSION_SOURCES})

# Link OpenSSL in both the static library as the loadable extension
target_link_libraries(${EXTENSION_NAME} OpenSSL::SSL OpenSSL::Crypto)
target_link_libraries(${LOADABLE_EXTENSION_NAME} OpenSSL::SSL OpenSSL::Crypto)
input.new_string:
include_directories(src/include)

# --- libjq (built from the ./jq submodule via its own autotools build) ---
include(ExternalProject)

set(JQ_SRC_DIR     ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/jq)
set(JQ_BUILD_DIR   ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/jq-build)
set(JQ_INSTALL_DIR ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/jq-install)
set(JQ_STATIC_LIB  ${JQ_INSTALL_DIR}/lib/libjq.a)

ExternalProject_Add(jq_external
  SOURCE_DIR        ${JQ_SRC_DIR}
  BINARY_DIR        ${JQ_BUILD_DIR}
  INSTALL_DIR       ${JQ_INSTALL_DIR}
  CONFIGURE_COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E chdir ${JQ_SRC_DIR} autoreconf -i
            COMMAND ${JQ_SRC_DIR}/configure
                      --prefix=${JQ_INSTALL_DIR}
                      --disable-shared --enable-static --with-pic
                      --disable-maintainer-mode --disable-docs
                      --without-oniguruma
  BUILD_COMMAND      ${CMAKE_MAKE_PROGRAM}
  INSTALL_COMMAND    ${CMAKE_MAKE_PROGRAM} install
  BUILD_BYPRODUCTS   ${JQ_STATIC_LIB}
)

# Make headers visible at configure time (the dir exists after install; create it
# now so include_directories doesn't warn on first run).
file(MAKE_DIRECTORY ${JQ_INSTALL_DIR}/include)

add_library(libjq STATIC IMPORTED GLOBAL)
add_dependencies(libjq jq_external)
set_target_properties(libjq PROPERTIES
  IMPORTED_LOCATION             ${JQ_STATIC_LIB}
  INTERFACE_INCLUDE_DIRECTORIES ${JQ_INSTALL_DIR}/include
)

set(EXTENSION_SOURCES src/jq_extension.cpp)

build_static_extension(${TARGET_NAME} ${EXTENSION_SOURCES})
build_loadable_extension(${TARGET_NAME} " " ${EXTENSION_SOURCES})

# Link OpenSSL and libjq in both the static library and the loadable extension
target_link_libraries(${EXTENSION_NAME}          OpenSSL::SSL OpenSSL::Crypto libjq)
target_link_libraries(${LOADABLE_EXTENSION_NAME} OpenSSL::SSL OpenSSL::Crypto libjq)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.5)

# Set extension name here
set(TARGET_NAME jq)

# DuckDB's extension distribution supports vcpkg. As such, dependencies can be added in ./vcpkg.json and then
# used in cmake with find_package. Feel free to remove or replace with other dependencies.
# Note that it should also be removed from vcpkg.json to prevent needlessly installing it..
find_package(OpenSSL REQUIRED)

set(EXTENSION_NAME ${TARGET_NAME}_extension)
set(LOADABLE_EXTENSION_NAME ${TARGET_NAME}_loadable_extension)

project(${TARGET_NAME})

set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD "17" CACHE STRING "C++ standard to enforce")
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)

include_directories(src/include)

set(EXTENSION_SOURCES src/jq_extension.cpp)

build_static_extension(${TARGET_NAME} ${EXTENSION_SOURCES})
build_loadable_extension(${TARGET_NAME} " " ${EXTENSION_SOURCES})

# Link OpenSSL in both the static library as the loadable extension
target_link_libraries(${EXTENSION_NAME} OpenSSL::SSL OpenSSL::Crypto)
target_link_libraries(${LOADABLE_EXTENSION_NAME} OpenSSL::SSL OpenSSL::Crypto)

install(
  TARGETS ${EXTENSION_NAME}
  EXPORT "${DUCKDB_EXPORT_SET}"
  LIBRARY DESTINATION "${INSTALL_LIB_DIR}"
  ARCHIVE DESTINATION "${INSTALL_LIB_DIR}")

content.content: The file /Users/tim_vergenz/code/personal/duckdb-jq/src/jq_extension.cpp has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text:
A few notes / decisions worth surfacing:

- **`--with-pic`** is the key flag — without it, linking `libjq.a` into the loadable `.so`/`.dylib` will fail on Linux with relocation errors.
- **`--without-oniguruma`** disables jq's regex filters (`test`, `match`, `sub`, `gsub`, etc.) but removes the external dep. If you want them, switch to `--with-oniguruma=builtin` (uses jq's vendored copy in `jq/modules/oniguruma`) — but you'll need to `git submodule update --init --recursive` inside `jq/` to pull it. Easiest to start without and add later if needed.
- **`--disable-maintainer-mode --disable-docs`** avoids needing flex/bison/python at build time. The checked-in `lexer.c`/`parser.c` are used as-is.
- **Host tooling required**: `autoconf`, `automake`, `libtool` need to be installed (`brew install autoconf automake libtool` on macOS).
- The submodule directory will get autotools-generated files in it (`configure`, `Makefile.in`, etc.) when `autoreconf` runs in-tree. If that bothers you, the alternative is copying the source tree into the build dir first — but the in-tree approach is simpler and the generated files are gitignored by jq's own `.gitignore`.

After this, do a clean configure to pick up the new ExternalProject. If `make` fails inside the jq build, the error will point at the autotools step — common ones are missing `libtool` on the host.
